Interface OutboxDialect

public interface OutboxDialect
Dialect SPI for outbox-jdbc. A dialect encapsulates every database-specific decision the publisher must make: the INSERT SQL (including the idempotency clause), the column-type bindings for non-trivial JDBC types (UUID, JSON/JSONB, TIMESTAMP WITH TIMEZONE), and the translation from SQLException to a typed OutboxException subtype.

Implementations are auto-discovered through OutboxDialectProvider via ServiceLoader. Applications may also pass an explicit dialect to the JdbcOutbox builder to bypass auto-detection.

Implementations must be thread-safe — a single dialect instance is shared across every publish / publishAll call on the JdbcOutbox that resolved it. The OutboxInsert handles the dialect returns from prepareInsert(Connection, TableRef) are not required to be thread-safe; a fresh handle is acquired for every call.

Since:
0.1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Set<DialectCapability>
    capabilities()
    Returns the dialect's optional capabilities.
    OutboxInsert
    prepareInsert(Connection connection, TableRef table)
    Prepares an OutboxInsert handle for the given table.
    OutboxException
    translate(SQLException ex, String contextMessage)
    Translates a JDBC failure into a typed OutboxException subtype, following a SQLState classification scheme that maps integrity violations, transient connectivity errors, data errors, and configuration errors onto the appropriate sealed subtype.
    default void
    validate(OutboxEvent event)
    Validates that event fits this dialect's underlying column constraints (width, byte length, character set, etc.) and throws OutboxDataException otherwise.

  • Method Details

    • prepareInsert

      OutboxInsert prepareInsert(Connection connection, TableRef table) throws SQLException
      Prepares an OutboxInsert handle for the given table. The dialect owns the underlying PreparedStatement — including the SQL text, the idempotency clause, and the column-type bindings — and exposes only the OutboxInsert surface to the publisher.

      The returned handle is bound to connection; the publisher is responsible for closing it via try-with-resources before the connection commits.

      Parameters:
      connection - the JDBC connection the statement will be prepared on; never null.
      table - the target outbox table; never null.
      Returns:
      a fresh OutboxInsert handle the publisher will drive for one batch.
      Throws:
      SQLException - if preparing the statement fails.
      Since:
      0.2.0

    • translate

      OutboxException translate(SQLException ex, String contextMessage)
      Translates a JDBC failure into a typed OutboxException subtype, following a SQLState classification scheme that maps integrity violations, transient connectivity errors, data errors, and configuration errors onto the appropriate sealed subtype.
      Parameters:
      ex - the original SQLException; never null.
      contextMessage - human-readable context to include in the exception message.
      Returns:
      the matching OutboxException subtype.

    • capabilities

      Set<DialectCapability> capabilities()
      Returns the dialect's optional capabilities.
      Returns:
      the set of supported DialectCapability values.

    • validate

      default void validate(OutboxEvent event)
      Validates that event fits this dialect's underlying column constraints (width, byte length, character set, etc.) and throws OutboxDataException otherwise. Called by JdbcOutbox immediately before binding each row so callers get a fail-fast error before the SQL is ever sent.

      The default implementation is a no-op so existing dialect implementations remain source compatible. PostgreSQL's dialect overrides this to enforce the byte-length limits of the reference outbox schema (see AGENTS.md > Table contract). Adopters running a customized schema with wider columns can either skip the outbox-jdbc default dialect or supply their own OutboxDialect that relaxes the check.

      Since 0.4.0 width/range failures surface as OutboxDataException (a leaf of the sealed OutboxException hierarchy) instead of IllegalArgumentException, so callers can rely on a single catch (OutboxException e) block.

      Parameters:
      event - the event about to be persisted; never null.
      Throws:
      OutboxDataException - if a field violates this dialect's constraints.
      Since:
      0.3.0
